segments.caption: The video opens in black and white, showing a group of young performers in a rehearsal space, dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black dresses or tops with accessories like statement necklaces. The scene begins with a close-up of a male performer singing passionately, gesturing with his arms, while a female performer beside him mirrors his energy with expressive hand movements. The camera pans across the group, capturing synchronized movements and interactions, suggesting a choreographed a cappella performance. On-screen text identifies the group as 'Pitch Slapped' and the event as the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella,' with the Berklee College of Music logo visible. As the video progresses, the black-and-white filter transitions to full color, revealing more details: the wooden floor, arched doorways, and a large clock on the wall. The group performs dynamic, synchronized dance moves, including spins and coordinated arm gestures, with the lead male and female performers often at the forefront. The atmosphere is energetic and theatrical, with the performers fully engaged in their routine. The lighting remains consistent, highlighting the performers against the neutral-toned studio backdrop.

segments.caption: The video transitions from a live performance to a closing credits sequence. Initially, three small video thumbnails appear at the top of the screen, each showing different musical performances: a woman singing into a microphone with a 'bim' logo, a man singing into a vintage-style microphone on stage, and hands playing a keyboard. Below these thumbnails, white text boxes read 'Credits', 'Subscribe', and 'Watch more from Berklee', serving as a call-to-action. The thumbnails cycle through various performers, including a woman singing with a man in a hat, a man singing on stage with a spotlight, and a drummer playing in a dimly lit setting. The scene then fades to black, and white text credits roll, listing song titles such as 'Take Our Time', 'Radio Song', 'Ain't it Fun', and 'Take Back the Night', along with lead vocalists and arrangers. Additional credits follow, naming the members of 'Pitch Slapped' by vocal range, choreographers, recording engineers, videographer, and mastering studio. The Berklee College of Music logo is no longer visible, and the environment shifts entirely to a black screen with text, marking the end of the performance and the beginning of the closing sequence.
